message:
feat(fuzz): add fuzz_setjmp + fuzz_dlfcn + file bd-qhnxc pthread_cancel abort

## FULL REVIEW MODE finding

bd-qhnxc [HIGH]: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_abi_test
deterministically SIGABRTs at `test cancel_running_thread` — repros on
BOTH default parallelism AND `--test-threads=1`, so this defeats the
serial-mode workaround. The victim thread sets DEFERRED cancel state +
sleeps at a cancellation point; the main thread pthread_cancels it
after 20 ms. Abort fires during the cancel/unwind path.

Diagnosis candidates: SIGCANCEL handler not installed before
pthread_cancel; cancel-unwind trips an assertion in TLS-dtor code
landed by a581f0ac; free on an internal struct allocated through a
different allocator (same `free(): invalid size` pattern as
bd-g2bd5/bd-ye0f4). Filed via br create --no-db --no-auto-flush
--no-auto-import per instruction.

## Fuzz gap drain — bd-dvr22 priorities 5 + 6

**fuzz_setjmp.rs** (priority-5, 2 op variants):
- SetjmpRoundTrip: setjmp(env)→0; longjmp(env, val); setjmp returns
  `val` (or 1 if val==0 per POSIX §7.13.2.1). Toggles setjmp/_setjmp.
- SigsetjmpRoundTrip: same with sigsetjmp(env, savemask), longjmp or
  siglongjmp.

Design: all round-trips stay within the fuzz_target body's SAME stack
frame — never unwind across libFuzzer's own setjmp boundary (known-
risky pattern). Fresh `Box<JmpBuf>` ([u64; 64], 16-byte aligned) per
op, so a stale env from iteration N-1 can never be mis-used. Assertion
fires iff setjmp's second return value doesn't match the
expected_longjmp_return(val) POSIX remap.

**fuzz_dlfcn.rs** (priority-6, 7 op variants):
- dlopen(NULL) / dlopen from a small allowlist (libc.so.6, libm.so.6,
  libdl.so.2, libpthread.so.0) — the fuzzer never drives the filename
  pointer, so it can't load attacker-chosen .so paths.
- dlsym with a known-good symbol set OR a fuzzer-supplied NUL-stripped
  byte string (exercises the parser without loading foreign code).
- dlvsym implicitly covered by dlsym (they share the lookup path).
- dlclose with Live/Stale tracking — double-close must return 0/-1,
  never corrupt the internal handle table.
- dlerror contract: returned message, if non-null, must be a
  bounded-scan valid C string.
- dladdr invoked on a dlsym-resolved address: rc ∈ {0, 1}.

Both clean under cargo build + cargo clippy --no-deps -- -D warnings.
Global locks serialize iterations.

bd-dvr22 priority remaining: pthread (P7 — TSan), c11threads (P8 —
TSan). Both large-surface, need separate TSan campaigns.

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -80,6 +80,16 @@ name = "fuzz_fortify"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_fortify.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_setjmp"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_setjmp.rs"
+doc = false
+
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_dlfcn"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_dlfcn.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_resolver"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_resolver.rs"
